title: Estimating mass (ounces and pounds)
description: Practice estimating the mass of real life objects using US customary units.
topic_path: Topic B: Problem solving with measurement

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is the closest to the mass of a banana?**

[[☃ radio 1]]


[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
A slice of bread has a mass of about $1$ ounce:

[[☃ image 1]]

A soccer ball has a mass of about $1$ pound:

[[☃ image 2]]
Is the mass of a banana closer to $4$ slices of bread (ounces) or $4$ soccer balls (pounds)?
The mass of a banana is about $4$ ounces.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is the closest to the mass of an elephant?**

[[☃ radio 1]]


[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
A soccer ball has a mass of about $1$ pound: 
[[☃ image 1]]
Is the mass of an elephant closer to $60$ soccer balls or $6{,}000$ soccer balls?
The mass of an elephant is about $6{,}000$ pounds.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the most reasonable unit to measure the mass of an average adult.**  


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A slice of bread has a mass of about $1$ ounce: 

[[☃ image 1]]

A soccer ball has a mass of about $1$ pound: 

[[☃ image 2]]
Is it more reasonable to measure how many 'bread slices heavy' a person is or how many 'soccer balls heavy' she is?
Pounds is the most reasonable unit to measure the mass of an average adult.

Pounds is the most reasonable unit to measure the mass of a refrigerator.
